comment.body:
My objective of being here is to learn how to read MM and options flow data to better improve my intraday trading success and, potentially, improve swing trading success up to 3 months out (if applicable).

@Terrence Chan made this post yesterday about your service and I agree that this encapsulates my experience so far. Here it is:

“My 2 cents. The consistent thing that you can be sure is actionable that you will get from this service are the intraday levels and the dealer flow analysis.

Anything beyond a day but probably a good read on the week are her weekly pivots and then goes to longer positioning like JPM collar or iron condor man. 

And then outside of this - what you’re getting is interpreting of the fed actions, the geo politician actions, and possibly quarter end options expiry/vixperation analysis. 

It’s like the layers to the onion. There are some core things that are very much actionable. But the speculative outer layers you have to understand are stabs at predictions the future that Alma is making her best interpretation of 
for our benefit.”

I agree that your analysis seems to be most actionable on an intraday basis. Yet you are saying that the macro understanding of the larger story is the most important. My issue here is, like Terrence alluded to, that the “outer layers” of the onion are the most “speculative” and “subjective”. It’s the least quantifiable analysis that can be applied accurately from a timing and price perspective. And therefore is the least actionable.

So are we “muddying up the waters” by talking about this when your analysis is most applicable and actionable on a shorter time frame?

I thought you are here to teach us to fish… yet there are times where you want to be treated as an analyst service where your skills cannot be replicated or taught directly. Just seems like sometimes we’re are getting lost in what the purpose of this Substack is in the first place.

I can appreciate your geopolitical analysis and your commentary on what funds are doing, but unless you continue to provide that for as long as I am trading, it really doesn’t help me outside of the next year or so you choose to have this Substack. I guess you are showing to think critically or to try to piece the puzzle together, but that is so broad and subjective that somebody could get caught in their own feedback loop. You just said you have acquaintances in the industry to bounce your ideas off of to see if you are on the right track. But, as I mentioned, many of your subscribers don’t. 

Does this make sense?

comment.body:
I made this call all on my own last Friday… I’m a genius… they call me Ye… 😂 

So me saying that I want to learn to fish and not become reliant on macro analysis that can’t really be replicated is somehow me wanting to be fed regurgitated fish from Alma like she’s a mother Eagle in the Alaskan tundra. I don’t think you ever read what I write when you respond to me.

I have read the education materials many times. (And to be honest, please keep this a secret, I still don’t grasp it fully… believe it or not. Please don’t expose me.)
